_README: Lighting config for ShowRunner — read at launch by the (separate) Lighting module. Editing this file NEVER affects the audio show. Keys starting with _ are notes and are ignored by the app. Set 'enabled' to false to turn lighting off entirely.
_VENUE: CC De Factorij (Theaterzaal Maupertuis), Zaventem. This patch is the FINAL venue lighting plot: Lighting_Plot/Lions patchv01.pdf + Lions v01.pdf (drawing 'Lions v01', Bruno Peysmans, 11/6/2026). Universe 1 = front catwalk 2KW dimmers (addr 2–24) + house lights 501–506 + MDG ATMe hazer 509–512 (house/hazer NOT patched here — venue-run). Universe 2 = 8× Robe Spiider Mode 3 33ch + 2× Robe T1 Profile Mode 3 53ch. Universe 3 = 7× Robert Juliat Dalis 860 MKII Mode 2 22ch (plot labels Dalis 4–10, the upstage cyc row). Light network: 192.168.202.0/24, DHCP, protocol sACN.
_POSITIONS: From the plot (top view): Spiider1/2 downstage pair at ±2 m; Spiider3/4 wide pair at ±7 m; Spiider5/6 wide pair at ±7 m one batten upstage; Spiider7/8 upstage inner pair at ±3 m; T1L/T1R upstage-centre back-key specials over the piano; Dalis 4–10 one even row washing the cyc. Mirrored pairs: 1↔2, 3↔4, 5↔6, 7↔8 (right pan = 1 − left pan).

_CONFIRM.modes: Modes come from the venue's own patch sheet and the profiles are built from the OFFICIAL charts (Robe Spiider chart v2.3 Mode 3; Robe T1 Profile chart v2.1 Mode 3; RJ Dalis 860 manual DN41077600-B Mode 2). Remaining risk is paper vs reality: on the day confirm each fixture's PATCHED mode matches the plot, then tap ARM MOVERS (Spiiders + T1s stay dark until armed). The Dalis profile is live (no arming) — sanity-check its colours at soundcheck.
_CONFIRM.network: 'mode' = 'multicast' (onto the venue sACN lighting LAN, 239.255.x.x) or 'unicast' (to a node IP via 'unicastHost'). Venue: light network 192.168.202.0/24 with DHCP, default protocol sACN. CRITICAL on a multi-NIC Mac: 'interface' pins which NIC multicast LEAVES by — without it macOS sends sACN out the default route (usually Wi-Fi/house LAN) while the rig listens on the lighting LAN, so packets go out but nothing arrives. Accepts an interface name ('en14'), a subnet PREFIX ('192.168.202' — DHCP-robust, recommended, binds to whatever .202.x address the Mac gets), or a literal local IP. Empty = OS default route. Startup log prints 'multicast egress pinned to <ip> (<nic>)' — verify it names the lighting NIC. (See also showtime.sh: it forces Tailscale accept-routes off so the tunnel can't swallow 224.0.0/4.)
_CONFIRM.front_catwalk: Universe 1 addr 2–24 are the venue's 2KW Profile/PC front-catwalk dimmers, driven as ONE level ('FrontWash'). Their focus is the venue's — ask for an even piano-centred front wash.
_CONFIRM.hazer: MDG ATMe on universe 1: 509 heater (always on), 510 pressure, 511 fog on/off, 512 fan. Deliberately NOT patched — agree haze level with the venue tech (the EDM beam looks want haze).

_STAGE: This is a piano show: the mover rig is designed around the piano's position. 'pianoPan'/'pianoTilt' (0..1, 0.5 = stage centre) are the single ROOT — the plot puts the piano centre, slightly left, so pianoPan defaults a touch under 0.5. Every mover's aim is then expressed as a deviation from that root scaled by ONE knob, 'stretch': finalAim = root + (authoredAim - root) * stretch, clamped 0..1. stretch = 1 leaves all the authored timelines/cues exactly as designed; lower it toward 0 to pull the whole mover rig in onto the piano (0 = every beam on the piano); above 1 exaggerates the spread. 'invertTilt' flips every mover's tilt about mechanical centre (tilt -> 1-tilt) for when the rig hangs inverted vs the authored convention (high tilt = toward cyc): with it ON, beams that pointed upstage/over the band come down onto the piano at the FRONT of the stage. Aim (pan/tilt) only — colour, intensity, zoom and the non-movers (FrontWash, Dalis) are never touched. Move the piano on the day => change pianoPan/pianoTilt here in ONE place; the live rig AND the stage preview's focal point both follow.
_T1_BACKKEY: 't1BackKeyTilt' PINS both upstage T1 specials to one ABSOLUTE tilt (0..1 raw DMX), ignoring the timeline sweep AND invertTilt. They hang far upstage by the screen as back-key specials on the piano; from up there no authored sweep tilt reaches forward enough to land on the downstage piano (it grazes the cyc/screen in either invert sense), so we lock them. SHOW-DAY DIAL: nudge this ONE number until the back-key beam sits on the piano — HIGHER = more downstage/forward (toward the audience & piano), LOWER = back toward the screen. Remove the field (or set null) to let the T1s sweep with the timeline like the Spiiders.
_PIECES_NOTE: Keys MUST match the 'order' strings in showrunner.json (running order: P1-P3, 1-5, S1... 4-5, 6A, 6B, S2, 7A, 7B, 8, 9A, 9B, S3, 10-12, S4, 13, E1-E3). S1-S4 are Lionel's speaking cues — a warm, calm look. The nightmare pieces split into a live SOLO/REG half (6A Torrent=calm white, 7A Für Elise=pink/red+teal, 9A Moonlight=blue moonlight — no audio, 'auto' self-driven look in the reg palette) then the NIGHTMARE backing half (6B, 7B, 9B — audio 'edm' timeline). Canon (8) is EDM throughout (no A/B). P1-P3 are the pre-show academy slides — intentionally NOT configured here: Lionel isn't at the board yet and the venue runs house lighting for the students, so ShowRunner holds the current look (pushes no DMX) when they fire.
